title: Software
text: PeakSEA is an R package to compute rank-based enrichment of genomic regions in a set of peaks. It is designed to be used with ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and other peak-based genomic data. The package takes advantage of the GSEA method to compute enrichment scores for a given set of pre-ranked peaks against reference peak sets of interest (e.g. conserved regions, GWAS loci), allowing users to identify genomic regions that are significantly enriched in their data.

title: Software
text: PeakSEA is an R package to compute rank-based enrichment of genomic regions in a set of peaks. It is designed to be used with ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and other peak-based genomic data. The package takes advantage of the GSEA method to compute enrichment scores for a given set of pre-ranked peaks against reference peak sets of interest (e.g. conserved regions, GWAS loci), allowing users to identify genomic regions that are significantly enriched in their data.

title: Software
text:
bigQTL
bigQTL is an R package extending bigsnpr to perform fast and efficient cis-QTL mapping on genomic datasets. Its main feature is its ability to do step-wise and all-but-one conditioning efficiently across many molecular traits.

title: Software
text:
plyranges
I am a contributor to the plyranges package as part of the tidyomics consortium. plyranges is an R package that provides a grammar for manipulating genomic ranges in a tidy and consistent manner. It allows users to perform common operations on genomic ranges, such as filtering, joining, and summarizing, using the tidy syntax.

title: Pierre-Paul Axisa
text:
I am a computational genomics researcher at the Center for Basic Metabolic Research (CBMR) at the University of Copenhagen. I use integrative genetics and genomics approaches to study human physiology in health and disease, with a particular interest in variant-to-function genomics. I have a long background studying immune system dysfunction in the context of autoimmunity and cancer. I am currently working on projects related to the genetics of metabolic diseases and inflammation, notably the role of adipose tissue.
I am also passionate about anything related to the R programming language, especially the tidyverse ecosystem in the context of genomic data analysis. I contribute to the tidyomics project and other open-source software projects, and I am always looking for ways to improve the accessibility and reproducibility of computational biology research.
